questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 107-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT the reason why Fuzz has become the official ambassador of his department?
options: He has attracted plenty of young local residents.
He has become everyone’s favorite in his unit.
He has effectively relieved the policemen’s stress.

He has a way with the other police dogs in his department. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 37-41 題。 As we all know, coffee is one of the most popular drinks in every corner of the world, certainly including Taiwan. On average, people in Taiwan consume 2.17 billion cups of coffee every year; indeed, almost every main street in our major cities is lined with coffee shops. Moreover, walking into a bookshop, one will surely find dozens of books on coffee. Last but not least, more and more universities have offered professional coffee-related courses. All these indicate that coffee in Taiwan today isn’t just a drink but an important part of our local culture. As coffee is now so popular in Taiwan, most of us know a thing or two about it. For example, almost everyone knows that coffee is rich in caffeine, which can keep us awake and excited. Moreover, heavy coffee drinkers may get addicted to caffeine. That is, they may have a headache or get dizzy if their body doesn’t absorb sufficient caffeine. Hence, it is generally suggested that we should ease up on coffee. A while ago, there was a theory spreading on the Internet, and it might give us another reason why we should drink less coffee. According to this theory, the process of brewing coffee may produce a chemical compound called acrylamide, too much of which will get us to catch cancer! Frankly speaking, this theory did create a panic among our local coffee lovers when it was first made public. Luckily, a group of WHO experts soon released an official statement proving it false. They claimed that after years of research, they had concluded that coffee actually contains several ingredients that can significantly help to reduce our risk of getting cancer. As for acrylamide, it may be generated as a by-product while we are brewing coffee. However, there is such a small amount of it that it won’t pose any serious danger. Thus, it turns out to be that this theory is nothing but a false alarm. It means that our local coffee lovers can continue enjoying their favorite drink, as long as they remember to keep their habit under control. Most of all, it looks like coffee may be here in Taiwan to stay, now that it has very little to do with our getting cancer.
questions.explanation: 正解：(D)；來源：107-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.subject: 英文

questions.explanation: 正解：(B)；來源：107-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.subject: 英文

Today it’s common knowledge that the more caffeine one has, the better it is for him or her.
Coffee isn’t a dangerous drink because caffeine, its main ingredient, can reduce our risk of getting cancer.
questions.explanation: 正解：(A)；來源：107-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf

questions.q: From the essay, we can know that.
options: coffee can reduce our risk of getting cancer because it’s rich in acrylamide
with the books promoting coffee, coffee shops are everywhere in Taiwan
the theory on the Internet isn’t the reason why we should drink less coffee
according to the WHO experts, there is no acrylamide in brewed coffee at all

questions.subject: 英文

questions.q: If you ease up on a food, you.
options: will be able to find it more easily
have already been familiar with it
merely have a limited amount of it
try to eat as much of it as you can
questions.explanation: 當你對某種食物適可而止,你 。 字遣詞沒有不當之處。 (A) 將較容易找到這種食物 能適切表達原文題意,對題材有良好的掌握, (B) 已經對這種食物很熟悉 3 句子結構及連貫性大致得當。文法及標點、用 (C) 僅限量食用這種食物 字遣詞有少數不當之處,但不妨礙題意之表達。 (D) 儘可能多吃這種食物 僅能部分表達原文題意,句子結構不良或有

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 107-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT the author’s point in the last paragraph?
options: Our local coffee lovers shouldn’t worry about getting cancer.
We should know that drinking too much coffee is bad for us.
This theory is no false alarm at all, so it could create a panic.

Coffee will remain an important part of the Taiwanese culture. 第二部分:非選擇題 ( 第 I 至 III 題,每題 6 分,共 18 分 ) I. 填充 說明: ▲請依據中文提示,將試題內空格  、  答案之完整單字 ( 含提示之字首 ),分別作答於答 案卷之作答欄  、  之指定範圍內以完成句子。 ▲請勿抄題,每格限填一字,超過一字者視為錯誤,不予計分。
questions.explanation: 下列何者「不是」作者在末段的觀點? 2 誤,且大多難以理解並缺乏連貫性。文法及標 (A) 本地咖啡的愛好者不應擔心得癌症。 點、用字遣詞有失當之處。 (B) 我們應當明瞭喝咖啡過量對我們有害。 無法表達原文題意,句子沒有結構概念及連貫 (C) 這個理論根本不是虛驚一場,所以能夠造成恐慌。 1 性,無法理解。文法及標點、用字遣詞之錯誤 (D) 咖啡將一直是臺灣文化中的重要部份。 甚多。 (1) 空白 ( 未書寫文字 ) 。 第二部分:非選擇題 (2) 文字瑣碎、形同未答。 0 I. 填充 (3) 只抄題目或其他試題。 (4) 內容完全離題或錯誤。
